---
title: "`goFlux`"
code-block-bg: true
---


 A wrapper function to calculate GHG fluxes from static chamber measurements.
 Calculates linear ( list(list("LM.flux")) ) and non-linear
 fluxes (Hutchinson and Mosier model;  list(list("HM.flux")) ),
 from a variety of greenhouse gases (
 list(list("html"), list(list("CO<sub>2</sub>")), list(list(list("CO[2]"), list("ASCII")))) ,
 list(list("html"), list(list("CH<sub>4</sub>")), list(list(list("CH[4]"), list("ASCII")))) ,
 list(list("html"), list(list("N<sub>2</sub>O")), list(list(list("N[2]O"), list("ASCII")))) ,
 list(list("html"), list(list("NO<sub>2</sub>")), list(list(list("NO[2]"), list("ASCII")))) , NO,
 list(list("html"), list(list("NH<sub>3</sub>")), list(list(list("NH[3]"), list("ASCII")))) , CO, and
 list(list("html"), list(list("H<sub>2</sub>O")), list(list(list("H[2]O"), list("ASCII")))) ).


## Description

goFlux: a user-friendly GHG fluxes calculation tool

## Usage

```r


goFlux(

  dataframe,

  gastype,

  H2O_col = "H2O_ppm",

  prec = NULL,

  Area = NULL,

  offset = NULL,

  Vtot = NULL,

  Vcham = NULL,

  Pcham = NULL,

  Tcham = NULL,

  k.mult = 1,

  warn.length = 60,

  k.min = 0

)

```

## Arguments

| Parameter | Description |
|:----------|:--------------------------|
| `dataframe` | a data.frame containing gas measurements (see gastype below), water vapor measurements (see H2O_col below) and the following columns: UniqueID , Etime... |
| `gastype` | character string; specifies which column should be used for the flux calculations. Must be one of the following: "CO2dry_ppm", "CH4dry_ppb", "COdry_pp... |
| `H2O_col` | character string; specifies which column should be used to subtract the effect of water vapor in the chamber space. Default is H2O_col = "H2O_ppm" . I... |
| `prec` | numerical value; precision of the instruments. Units must be the same as gastype . With the default prec = NULL , instrument precision for each gas mu... |
| `Area` | numerical value; area of the soil surface inside the chamber html (cm2) (cm^2) ASCII . Alternatively, provide the column Area in dataframe if Area is ... |
| `offset` | (optional) numerical value; height between the soil surface and the chamber (cm). Alternatively, provide the column offset in dataframe if offset is d... |
| `Vtot` | numerical value; total volume inside the chamber, tubes, instruments, etc. (L). Alternatively, provide the column Vtot in dataframe if Vtot is differe... |
| `Vcham` | (optional) numerical value; volume inside the chamber, tubes and instruments (L). Alternatively, provide the column Vcham in dataframe if Vcham is dif... |
| `Pcham` | numerical value; pressure inside the chamber (kPa). Alternatively, provide the column Pcham in dataframe if Pcham is different between samples. If Pch... |
| `Tcham` | numerical value; temperature inside the chamber (°C). Alternatively, provide the column Tcham in dataframe if Tcham is different between samples. If T... |
| `k.mult` | numerical value; a multiplier for the allowed kappa-max. Default setting is no multiplier ( k.mult = 1 ). k.mult must be > 0 and =< 10. |
| `warn.length` | numerical value; limit under which a measurement is flagged for being too short ( nb.obs < warn.length ). |
| `k.min` | numerical value; a lower boundary value for kappa in the HM model. Default is k.min = 0 |

## Value


 Returns a data frame with 32 columns: a  list("UniqueID")  per
 measurement, 11 columns for the linear model results (linear flux estimate
 ( list(list("LM.flux")) ), initial gas concentration
 ( list("LM.C0") ), final gas concentration ( list("LM.Ct") ), slope of linear
 regression ( list("LM.slope") ), mean absolute error ( list("LM.MAE") ), root mean
 square error ( list("LM.RMSE") ), Akaike's information criterion corrected for
 small sample size ( list("LM.AICc") ), standard error ( list("LM.SE") ), relative
 standard error ( list("LM.se.rel") ), coefficient of determination ( list("LM.r2") ),
 and  list("p-value")  ( list("LM.p.val") )), 11 columns for the non-linear model
 results (non-linear flux estimate ( list(list("HM.flux")) ),
 initial gas concentration ( list("HM.C0") ), the assumed concentration of
 constant gas source below the surface ( list("HM.Ci") ), slope at  list("t=0") 
 ( list("HM.slope") ), mean absolute error ( list("HM.MAE") ), root mean square error
 ( list("HM.RMSE") ), Akaike's information criterion corrected for small sample
 size ( list("HM.AICc") ), standard error ( list("HM.SE") ), relative standard error
 ( list("HM.se.rel") ), coefficient of determination ( list("HM.r2") ), and curvature
 (kappa;  list("HM.k") )), as well as the minimal detectable flux
 ( list(list("MDF")) ), the precision of the instrument
 ( list("prec") ), the flux term ( list(list("flux.term")) ),
 kappa-max ( list(list("k.max")) ) and its multiplier ( list("k.mult") ),
 the g-factor (g.fact;  list(list("g.factor")) ), the number of
 observations used ( list("nb.obs") ) and the true initial gas concentration
 ( list("C0") ) and final gas concentration ( list("Ct") ).


## Details


 Flux estimate units are
 list(list("html"), list(list("µmol m-2s-1")), list(list(list("µmol m^{-2}s^{-1}"), list("ASCII")))) 
 (if initial concentration is ppm, e.g. CO2dry_ppm) and
 list(list("html"), list(list("nmol m-2s-1")), list(list(list("nmol m^{-2}s^{-1}"), list("ASCII")))) 
 (if initial concentration is ppb, e.g. CH4dry_ppb).
 
 The  list(list("goFlux"))  function calculates flux estimates
 from the linear model (LM) and the Hutchinson and Mosier model (HM). The HM
 model is a non-linear model, whose curvature is controlled by the parameter
 kappa. A large kappa returns a strong curvature. A maximum threshold for this
 parameter, kappa-max ( list(list("k.max")) ), can be calculated
 from the linear flux estimate ( list(list("LM.flux")) ), the
 minimal detectable flux ( list(list("MDF")) ) and the time of
 chamber closure. This limit of kappa-max is included in the
 list(list("goFlux"))  function, so that the non-linear flux
 estimate cannot exceed this maximum curvature. Inversely, one can set a
 minimal threshold for kappa: to allow for a log-like curvature, set
 list("k.min")  below 0 (ex. -1).
 
 Flux estimates and the  list(list("MDF"))  are calculated from
 list("Etime")  (in seconds). Make sure that chamber closure is indicated by
 list("Etime == 0")  and the first instance of  list("flag == 1")  per UniqueID.
 
 All flux estimates, including the  list(list("MDF")) , are obtained
 from the multiplication of the slope and the  list(list("flux.term")) ,
 which is used to correct for the dilution effect of water vapor and convert
 the flux units to obtain a term in nmol or
 list(list("html"), list(list("µmol m-2s-1")), list(list(list("µmol m^{-2}s^{-1}"), list("ASCII")))) .
 
 The argument  list("Area")  is in  list(list("html"), list(list("(cm2)")), list(list(list("(cm^2)"), list("ASCII")))) ,
 but the output units from  list(list("goFlux"))  are in
 list(list("html"), list(list("(m2)")), list(list(list("(m^2)"), list("ASCII")))) . This means that
 there is a factor of 10,000 to convert from
 list(list("html"), list(list("cm2")), list(list(list("cm^2"), list("ASCII")))) 
 to  list(list("html"), list(list("m2")), list(list(list("m^2"), list("ASCII")))) . This is important
 to take into account if one would provide something else than an  list("Area") 
 in  list(list("html"), list(list("(cm2)")), list(list(list("(cm^2)"), list("ASCII"))))  to the function.
 For example, with incubated soil samples, one may provide an amount of soil
 (kg) instead of an area in the column  list("Area") . To get the right units in
 that case, multiply the kilograms of soil by 10,000 to remove the conversion
 from  list(list("html"), list(list("cm2")), list(list(list("cm^2"), list("ASCII"))))  to
 list(list("html"), list(list("m2")), list(list(list("m^2"), list("ASCII")))) .
 
 In  list("gastype") , the gas species listed are the ones for which this package
 has been adapted. Please write to the maintainer of this package for
 adaptation of additional gases.
 
 list("warn.length")  is the limit below which the chamber closure time is
 flagged for being too short ( list("nb.obs < warn.length") ). Portable
 greenhouse gas analyzers typically measure at a frequency of 1 Hz. Therefore,
 for the default setting of  list("warn.length = 60") , the chamber closure time
 should be approximately one minute (60 seconds). If the number of
 observations is smaller than the threshold, a warning is printed: e.g. "Low
 number of observations: UniqueID X has 59 observations".


## Examples

```r


data(manID.UGGA)

CO2_flux <- goFlux(manID.UGGA, "CO2dry_ppm")

CH4_flux <- goFlux(manID.UGGA, "CH4dry_ppb")

H2O_flux <- goFlux(manID.UGGA, "H2O_ppm")



```

## See Also


 Look up the functions  list(list("MDF")) ,
           list(list("flux.term")) ,
           list(list("g.factor")) ,
           list(list("k.max")) ,
           list(list("HM.flux"))  and
           list(list("LM.flux"))  of this package for more
          information about these parameters.
